content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 3, 2010

Ariel Pierre Calonne
City Attorney
501 Poli Street
Ventura, CA 93002-0099

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our file No. I-10-095

Dear Mr. Calonne:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Ventura City Councilmember
James L. Monahan regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹ Please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore
offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law
conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1090.

QUESTION

Does Ventura City Councilmember James Monahan have a conflict of interest that would
bar him from participating in governmental decisions that involve both annexing a parcel of
property to an existing city project (and other decisions regarding that annexation) where his
property is over three miles from the potential annex?

CONCLUSION

No. Based on the facts you provided in both your letter and our telephone conversations,
Mr. Monahan's economic interest in his real property does not bar him from participating in
these decisions.

FACTS

You are the City Attorney for the City of Ventura and you write with the consent of City
Council Member James L. Monahan. Ventura is engaged in an on-going project including two

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. I-10-095
Page No. 2

distinct communities, the Westside and North Avenue Community Plan Project (the "Project").
The Project will establish new planning and zoning regulations for the areas within its
boundaries by creating a "form-based" zoning code. During our telephone conversations, you
explained that "form-based" zoning is made on more of a lot-by-lot basis than area-by-area in
most cases.

The city council will be deciding whether to support or oppose the area's LAFCO's
decision regarding annexing a parcel of land into the Project. The parcel in whole is about 800
acres and the city has already annexed 120 of those acres. Once the LAFCO decides whether to
annex the property (called "Cañada Larga"), several decisions regarding planning and zoning
will follow.

Mr. Monahan owns four parcels in Ventura that are located in the Westside community
that is included in the Project. The value of each is at least $2,000. The parcels are used for
light-industrial and commercial purposes. His parcels are 17,800 feet (over three miles) from the
potential annexation of Cañada Larga.

You have asked whether Mr. Monahan can participate in decisions related to the
annexation while the Project is on-going and when it is complete.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or
using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a
financial interest. A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision, within
the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect on one or more of the public official's economic interests. (Section 87103;
Regulation 18700(a).) The Commission has adopted an eight-step standard analysis for deciding
whether an individual has a disqualifying conflict of interest in a given governmental decision.

Steps One and Two: Is Mr. Monahan a "public official" making or participating in making
a governmental decision?

A "public official" is "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
government agency. . . ." (Section 82048.) Mr. Monahan is a public official by virtue of his
position on the Ventura City Council. Mr. Monahan will be asked to participate in the decision to
support or oppose the LAFCO's annexing the Cañada Larga parcel. Following that, the city
council will also be asked to make several decisions related to the Project. Mr. Monahan will
therefore be making, participating in making, or influencing a governmental decision. (See
generally Regulation 18702.)

File No. I-10-095
Page No. 3

Step Three: What are Mr. Monahan's Economic Interests?

Mr. Monahan has an economic interest in the real estate he owns in Ventura. He owns
four properties in the Westside area of Ventura, part of the Project. The Act states that a public
official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect
interest of $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b); Regulation 18703.2.) Mr. Monahan therefore has
an economic interest in this property.

Step Four: Are the economic interests directly or indirectly involved in the governmental
decisions?

Regulation 18704.2(a)(1) (copy enclosed) states, in pertinent part, that real property in
which a public official has an economic interest is directly involved in a governmental decision
if:

"The real property in which the official has an interest, or any part of
that real property, is located within 500 feet of the boundaries (or the
proposed boundaries) of the property which is the subject of the
governmental decision. For purposes of subdivision (a)(5), real
property is located 'within 500 feet of the boundaries (or proposed
boundaries) of the real property which is the subject of the
governmental decision' if any part of the real property is within 500
feet of the boundaries (or proposed boundaries) of the redevelopment
project area." ²

Mr. Monahan's property is 17,800 feet away from the property line of the potential
annex. For purposes of Regulation 18704.2(a)(1), his properties are not directly involved. While
there is a project occurring in the city, and one that the city would like to add to, you stated
during our telephone conversations that this project has not been designated a "redevelopment
plan" or "redevelopment project." Because Mr. Monahan's property is not within 500 feet and is
not otherwise "directly" involved, it is indirectly involved.³

Steps Five and Six: Will there be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on
Mr. Monahan's economic interest?

The financial effect of a governmental decision on real property that is indirectly
involved in the governmental decision is presumed not to be material. This presumption may be

___________________________
² "If a public official's economic interest is not directly involved in a governmental decision, it is indirectly
involved." (Regulation 18704(a).)

³ Regulation 18704.2(a)(2) discusses instances in which zoning or re-zoning would cause the property to be
"directly involved." Two points on this topic: first, for that to be the case, the public official's property must be the
property subject to the zoning and second, the decision at hand is whether to annex a property that is 3 miles away,
not what zoning designation to give a particular property.

File No. I-10-095
Page No. 4

rebutted by proof that there are specific circumstances regarding the governmental decision, its
financial effect, and the nature of the real property which make it reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material effect on the real property in which the public official has
an interest. (Regulation 18705.2(b)(1).) Examples of such specific circumstances include the
development potential or income producing potential of the real property, the use of the real
property and the character of the neighborhood, including substantial effects on traffic, view,
privacy, intensity of use, noise levels, and air emissions. Here, none of the circumstances set
forth in Regulation 18705.2(b) appear to be present to rebut the presumption of non-materiality.
Consequently, based on the facts provided, we see no evidence that there would be a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect of the annexation or decisions about the annexation of
Cañada Larga to the Project.

We understand that the city council will be having future discussions regarding the
annexation and the Project. You are welcome to inquire again should an issue arise.

Steps Seven and Eight: Does this governmental decision come within any exception to the
conflict-of-interest rules?

The steps above revealed that Mr. Monahan is not barred from participating in a vote on
the Cañada Larga annexation or other decisions regarding the annexation. Even if an exception
was applicable, and none based on your facts, none is necessary.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division

HMR:jgl

bar him from participating in governmental decisions that involve both annexing a parcel of
property to an existing city project (and other decisions regarding that annexation) where his
property is over three miles from the potential annex?

CONCLUSION: No' Based on the facts you provided in both your letter and our telephone conversations,
Mr' Monahan's economic interest in his real property do., not bar him from participating in
these decisions.
